macro_summary: Markets enter a historically dense event week with FOMC on July 29 (hold probability ~83-85%), a mega-cap earnings cluster (MSFT July 29, AAPL/AMZN July 30), and live geopolitical risk from US-Iran conflict driving Brent crude above $100/barrel. Semiconductor global sales surged 104% YoY through May 2026, underpinning the AI infrastructure narrative. The S&P 500 sits near 7,499 with 88% of Q2 reporters beating estimates so far, but elevated rates and geopolitical uncertainty argue for defined-risk structures over outright equity exposure this week.
screening_note: 21 independent overnight screening runs analyzed. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. 6 tickers qualified. Ideas ranked by appearance count descending.
key_events: MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings: July 29, 2026 after close (consensus $87.7B revenue / $4.24 EPS)
AAPL Q3 FY2026 earnings: July 30, 2026 after close
FOMC rate decision: July 28-29, 2026 (hold expected ~83-85% probability)
SKHY (SK Hynix ADR) first Nasdaq earnings report: July 29, 2026
NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings: August 26, 2026 (MSFT Azure guide July 29 is key read-through)
SK Hynix Cheongju AI memory packaging plant expansion announcement: today
Brent crude above $101/barrel on Strait of Hormuz escalation; US crude reserves at 43-year low

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in all 21 overnight screens with unanimous high conviction — the strongest signal of the digest. The stock is down ~20% YTD and trades at ~20-22x forward earnings, its cheapest multiple since 2023, against a 97-analyst consensus with an average price target of $589-592 implying ~56% upside. July 29 earnings set a hard catalyst with Azure AI growth and Copilot monetization as the primary beat/raise variables, and the bar to beat is considered low after multiple compression.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings report July 29, 2026 after close; consensus $87.7B revenue / $4.24 EPS
ideas.edge: Consensus avg analyst target $589-592 vs. current depressed level implying ~56% upside; forward multiple at 21-22x NTM is cheapest since 2023; 97 analysts majority-rated Strong Buy/Buy
ideas.bull_case: Azure AI acceleration and Copilot monetization drive a beat-and-raise, catalyzing multiple re-expansion toward the historical ~30x forward P/E and rapid closure of the ~56% analyst target gap.
ideas.bear_case: Elevated capex from AI infrastructure buildout compresses near-term free cash flow, and any softness in Azure growth guidance causes a miss-and-lower that extends the YTD drawdown.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 21 overnight screens as the highest-visibility new AI-memory listing on Nasdaq, still in active price-discovery after IPO pricing at $149 just two weeks ago. The stock trades around $165, representing a ~70% gap to the average 12-month analyst target of $281.67, with a hard July 29 inaugural Nasdaq earnings catalyst and management guidance that the global memory shortage will persist beyond 2030. Today's announced Cheongju AI memory packaging plant expansion adds a near-term structural catalyst.
ideas.catalyst: First Nasdaq earnings report July 29, 2026; Cheongju AI memory packaging plant expansion announced today
ideas.edge: 12-month analyst consensus target $281.67 vs. ~$165 last close = ~70% implied upside; IPO priced at $149 two weeks ago, still in price-discovery phase with heavy institutional volume support
ideas.bull_case: Strong HBM3/HBM4 revenue in the inaugural print, combined with AI data-center demand commentary, accelerates institutional accumulation and compresses the 70% target gap rapidly in the post-IPO window.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO volatility is extreme, options liquidity is nascent, and any earnings miss or softer memory ASP outlook could erase the recent gains above IPO price with limited hedging infrastructure available.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 18 of 21 overnight screens, flagged as a pre-earnings dip opportunity ahead of the July 30 print. The stock has pulled back ~9% to ~$284 while Citi and HSBC both raised price targets in July to $365-366, and a Bloomberg-reported fall 2026 Mac AI overhaul provides a product cycle catalyst beyond the immediate quarter. Services at $31B/quarter with 32% margins provides an earnings floor while the AI product narrative supports multiple.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings report July 30, 2026 after close; fall 2026 Mac AI overhaul as secondary product catalyst
ideas.edge: Citi ($365) and HSBC ($366) raised targets in July vs. ~$284 current price implying ~28% upside; Services segment at $31B/qtr with 32% margins provides downside floor; two fresh Buy-side upgrades within the past two weeks
ideas.bull_case: Services beat and AI Mac cycle commentary drive multiple re-expansion toward analyst consensus, with the two fresh upgrades providing near-term institutional buying support into and after the print.
ideas.bear_case: At ~39.5x forward P/E, any iPhone unit miss or margin compression from AI infrastructure costs triggers a selloff, and a hawkish FOMC the prior day (July 29) could compress high-multiple tech broadly.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 17 of 21 overnight screens as the highest-conviction AI infrastructure beneficiary in the digest. The stock trades ~$202.78 against a 68-analyst consensus target of $302.73 (~49% implied upside) with 85% Buy ratings, while hyperscalers are collectively guiding $600B+ in 2026 AI capex. The MSFT earnings print July 29 (Azure capex guidance) and AAPL July 30 serve as sequential read-throughs before the definitive August 26 earnings catalyst, creating a staggered opportunity to accumulate via defined-risk spreads.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26, 2026; MSFT Azure capex guide July 29 and AAPL/AMZN commentary July 30 as near-term read-throughs
ideas.edge: 68-analyst consensus target $302.73 vs. ~$202.78 current = ~49% implied upside; Blackwell GPU supply up 40% in Q2 per KeyBanc; global chip sales +104% YoY (May 2026 SIA data); 85% of analysts rate Buy
ideas.bull_case: MSFT Azure and hyperscaler capex guidance confirms sustained Blackwell demand on July 29-30, driving NVDA higher as a read-through before the stock recaptures its own August 26 catalyst with a beat-and-raise print.
ideas.bear_case: Any hyperscaler capex pause signal or export restriction escalation between now and August 26 could compress the multiple sharply, and elevated IV heading into the print makes outright long stock ownership expensive on a drawdown.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 7 of 21 overnight screens as a macro-hedge and event-volatility vehicle rather than a directional bet. The July 28-30 window stacks the highest event density of the Q2 earnings season — FOMC decision, MSFT, AAPL, and META prints — into a 48-hour window, creating elevated implied volatility that favors options income structures over outright equity exposure. All 7 appearances rated medium conviction, consistent with its role as a hedge rather than primary idea.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC rate decision July 28-29 + MSFT/AAPL/META earnings cluster July 29-30, 2026
ideas.edge: Highest event-density 48-hour window of Q2 season; IV likely elevated into July 29-30 event cluster; Nasdaq-100 has maximum weighting to all reporting mega-caps; macro hold scenario at 3.5-3.75% historically supportive of tech multiple expansion
ideas.bull_case: Fed holds rates, MSFT and AAPL both beat, and IV crush post-event benefits a short-premium iron condor or call spread as the market absorbs the news constructively near all-time highs.
ideas.bear_case: A hawkish Fed surprise or a MSFT/AAPL guidance miss creates a double-negative event shock, sending QQQ sharply lower and rendering any upside-biased structure a full loss; put spreads would be the appropriate alternative hedge.

ideas.name: Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund
ideas.thesis: XLE appeared in 7 of 21 overnight screens as the cleanest liquid hedge against the active Iran-Strait of Hormuz geopolitical risk spike. Brent crude surged above $101/barrel today (+7.5%) while energy stocks bucked a broader market selloff of 1.3%, demonstrating explicit sector decoupling. XLE is up ~29-40% YTD as the best-performing S&P sector and carries integrated major FCF yields of 8-12% at current oil levels, with US crude reserves at a 43-year structural low underpinning the price floor.
ideas.catalyst: Ongoing US-Iran conflict with live Strait of Hormuz disruption; FOMC July 28-29 (September hike odds at 82%, oil-driven inflation primary driver)
ideas.edge: XLE +29-40% YTD, best-performing S&P sector; Brent >$101 today, WTI ~$93; integrated majors at 8-12% FCF yields; US emergency crude reserves at 43-year low; September Fed hike odds jumped from 53% to 82% in one week on oil-led inflation
ideas.bull_case: Strait of Hormuz disruption persists or escalates, Brent holds above $100, and the September Fed hike narrative entrenches, driving continued rotation into energy as an inflation hedge with no near-term geopolitical resolution in sight.
ideas.bear_case: A diplomatic de-escalation or ceasefire announcement reverses the oil spike rapidly, Brent retreats below $90, and the September hike repricing unwinds — leaving late energy buyers exposed to a sharp mean-reversion after a 40% YTD run.
